description: A top-down psychological sci-fi roguelite set on a decaying space station, built in Godot 4. Features a diegetic 4-pane CRT terminal UI, dual-layer gameplay (exploration + tactical turn-based combat), procedural floor generation, AP/MP systems, equipment-driven abilities, a Miasma stress mechanic, meta-progression hub, and multiple narrative endings.

userStories.title: Godot Project Initialization & 4-Pane UI Layout
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a Godot 4 project initialized with the correct structure so that all future systems have a foundation to build on.
acceptanceCriteria: Godot 4 project exists in C:/git/gmcp-test/ with project.godot
Main scene contains a 4-pane layout: Pane A (viewport, top-left), Pane B (nav-com map, top-right), Pane C (bio-status/gear, bottom-left), Pane D (data log, bottom-right)
Each pane is visually distinct with a dark terminal border/frame
Project runs without errors in Godot editor

userStories.title: CRT Shader Post-Processing Stack
userStories.description: As a player, I want the UI to look like a retro CRT terminal so that the diegetic sci-fi atmosphere is established from the first frame.
acceptanceCriteria: A CanvasLayer or SubViewport with a shader applies scanlines across the full screen
Spherical/barrel distortion visible on the screen edges
Phosphorus flicker effect implemented (subtle brightness oscillation)
Shader applies over all 4 panes simultaneously
Project runs without errors

userStories.title: Procedural Floor Generation
userStories.description: As a player, I want each run to have a unique station floor layout so that the game stays replayable.
acceptanceCriteria: A seed-based generator creates a set of rooms connected by hallways
Rooms are represented as nodes in a graph data structure
Generator places a start room, an elevator/exit room, and 6-12 intermediate rooms
Hallways between rooms are valid (no isolated rooms)
The same seed always produces the same layout
Project runs without errors

userStories.title: Nav-Com Map Panel (Pane B)
userStories.description: As a player, I want to see a schematic map of the station floor so that I can navigate strategically between rooms.
acceptanceCriteria: Pane B renders the generated floor as a node graph (Darkest Dungeon style)
Rooms display as boxes/nodes, hallways as connecting lines
Undiscovered rooms are hidden or shown as '?'
Current player location is highlighted
Elevator/exit room shows a 'ping' indicator once discovered
Map updates as player explores
Project runs without errors

userStories.title: Exploration Mode – Grid Movement in Pane A
userStories.description: As a player, I want to move freely through rooms and hallways in exploration mode so that I can discover the station at my own pace.
acceptanceCriteria: Pane A renders the current room as a tile grid
Player character sprite is visible and moves via keyboard input (WASD or arrow keys)
Moving to a room exit transitions the player to the connected room
Exploration mode is the default state (no combat active)
Hallway nodes can contain event triggers (locker, terminal placeholder)
Project runs without errors

userStories.title: Player Stats System (HP, Mental Health, AP, MP)
userStories.description: As a player, I want persistent stats that govern my survival so that my choices carry meaningful consequences.
acceptanceCriteria: Player has HP (max 100), Mental Health (max 100), AP (default 3), MP (default 5)
HP reaching 0 triggers a 'Reset' (run ends, returns to Hub)
Mental Health reaching 0 causes Pane D to display false information (hallucination text)
Stats are stored in a PlayerData resource/autoload
Stats reset at the start of each run
Project runs without errors

userStories.title: Bio-Status & Gear Panel (Pane C)
userStories.description: As a player, I want Pane C to display my current stats and equipped items so that I can make informed tactical decisions.
acceptanceCriteria: Pane C shows HP bar, Mental Health bar, current AP, current MP
Pane C shows 4 equipment slots: Weapon 1, Weapon 2, Core Suit, Utility Chip
Slots display the equipped item's name or 'EMPTY'
Stats update in real-time as they change
Project runs without errors

userStories.title: Data Log Panel (Pane D) – Scrolling Text Buffer
userStories.description: As a player, I want Pane D to output narrative and combat text so that I feel immersed in the station's world.
acceptanceCriteria: Pane D is a scrolling RichTextLabel or equivalent
New messages append to the bottom and the log scrolls down automatically
A DataLog autoload exposes a log(text: String) function callable from anywhere
Exploration events print flavor text (e.g., 'You enter the Hydroponics Bay. The air is thick.')
Combat results print math summaries (e.g., 'You fire. Hit! 12 damage.')
Project runs without errors

userStories.title: Tactical Combat Mode Transition
userStories.description: As a player, I want the game to lock down the room and switch to turn-based combat when I enter a room with enemies so that encounters feel tense and deliberate.
acceptanceCriteria: Entering a room with enemies triggers COMBAT_MODE
Room doors are sealed (exit blocked) until all enemies are defeated
Combat mode switches Pane A from free movement to turn-based grid interaction
Defeating all enemies unseals the doors and returns to EXPLORATION_MODE
A 'COMBAT ENGAGED' message appears in Pane D on transition
A 'ROOM CLEARED' message appears in Pane D on return to exploration
Project runs without errors

userStories.title: AP/MP Turn System & Basic Enemy AI
userStories.description: As a player, I want to spend Action Points and Movement Points each turn, and have enemies take their own turns, so that combat feels strategic.
acceptanceCriteria: Player turn begins with AP and MP refreshed to current max values
Moving one tile costs 1 MP; attacking costs 1 AP (base)
Ending turn (key press or AP/MP depleted) passes control to enemies
At least one enemy type exists that moves toward the player and performs a melee attack
Enemy turn ends and player turn begins again after all enemies act
AP/MP values update in Pane C in real-time
Combat actions log results to Pane D
Project runs without errors

userStories.title: Cover System (Partial & Full Cover)
userStories.description: As a player, I want to use pillars and crates for cover so that positioning matters in combat.
acceptanceCriteria: Rooms procedurally place pillar and crate props on the grid
A tile adjacent to a pillar grants Partial Cover (-25% enemy hit chance)
A tile behind a crate grants Full Cover (-50% enemy hit chance, blocks line-of-sight)
Line-of-sight calculation exists for ranged attacks/abilities
Cover status is reflected in hit chance calculations logged to Pane D
Project runs without errors

userStories.title: Loot System – Chests, Lockers & Items
userStories.description: As a player, I want to find items in the environment so that I can build my loadout during a run.
acceptanceCriteria: Cleared rooms can contain chest nodes; hallways can contain locker nodes
Interacting with a chest/locker reveals 1-3 random items
Items have a name, type (Weapon, Suit, Chip, Consumable), and stat block
Player can equip items to the Paper Doll slots in Pane C
Equipped items grant the stated ability/stat bonus
A Medkit item grants a 'Heal' action that restores 25 HP (costs 1 AP)
Project runs without errors

userStories.title: Miasma (Station Instability) Mechanic
userStories.description: As a player, I want a creeping instability meter to pressure me so that I can't stall indefinitely.
acceptanceCriteria: A Station Instability (Miasma) meter exists (0–100)
Miasma increases by 1 each player turn/move
At Miasma > 50: CRT shader intensifies (stronger scanlines/flicker) and Mental Health drains 1/turn
At Miasma > 75: enemies receive +1 MP per turn (move faster)
Miasma level displayed in Pane C or Pane D
Project runs without errors

userStories.title: The Hub (Cabin) – Between-Run Screen
userStories.description: As a player, I want a safe hub between runs so that I can prepare and feel progression across deaths.
acceptanceCriteria: Dying (HP=0) transitions to the Hub scene
Hub has a visual distinct from the station (cabin aesthetic)
Hub contains: The Stash (store 1 item), Neuro-Implants shop, 'Begin Run' button
Meta-currency 'Neural Scrip' is tracked persistently (saved to disk)
Completing a run (reaching an elevator) also transitions to the Hub with a score/scrip reward
Project runs without errors

userStories.title: Meta-Progression – Neuro-Implants
userStories.description: As a player, I want to spend Neural Scrip on permanent upgrades so that each run makes me progressively stronger.
acceptanceCriteria: At least 4 Neuro-Implants available: +Damage vs Mutants, +Mental Resistance, +Max HP, +Starting AP
Each implant has a Neural Scrip cost and is purchasable once
Purchased implants persist across runs (saved to disk)
Implant effects apply at run start
Implant shop UI shows name, description, cost, and purchased state
Project runs without errors

userStories.title: Blueprints & Terminal Interaction
userStories.description: As a player, I want to find blueprints and upload them at terminals so that I can unlock new items permanently.
acceptanceCriteria: Blueprint items exist and can be found as loot
Terminal nodes exist in hallways and can be interacted with
Interacting with a terminal while holding a Blueprint 'uploads' it (unlocks in Hub)
Terminals also display flavor lore text in Pane D
Uploaded blueprints appear in Hub as craftable/available items for future runs
Project runs without errors

userStories.title: Multiple Endings – Elevator & Exit Branches
userStories.description: As a player, I want multiple possible endings based on which path I take so that repeat runs feel meaningful.
acceptanceCriteria: Floor generator can designate elevator nodes with different destination tags: Bridge, Hangar, Source
Reaching the Bridge elevator triggers the 'Bridge Ending' (distress signal cutscene/text)
Reaching the Hangar elevator triggers the 'Hangar Ending' (escape pod text)
The Source path is locked behind requiring at least 3 specific lore items
Each ending displays unique text in Pane D and transitions to Hub with a label of the ending achieved
Project runs without errors
